description: Test cases for iOS list/table/collection view interaction: scrollToElement, manual scroll fallback, cell selection, cell swipe actions, and static structure checks (formerly ios-list-interaction)

evals.prompt: In an iOS app with a long UITableView, find and select the item with text 'Item 50' which is not currently visible on screen.
evals.expected_output:
Should successfully:
1. Call ui.scrollToElement with match='text' and value='Item 50' (2-7ms, no manual scroll needed)
2. Response should show found=true, targetPath, targetType='UILabel' or similar, container='UITableView'
3. Call ui.inspect to get a FRESH snapshot after scroll (old viewSnapshotID is stale)
4. Find item path in fresh targets array
5. Call ui_tap_and_inspect with fresh viewSnapshotID and item path (waitForStable=true for navigation animation)
6. Verify navigation to detail or selection state changed
7. Total time < 1 second

evals.prompt: Scroll down through an iOS collection view by half a screen using programmatic scroll, then verify new items are visible.
evals.expected_output:
Should successfully:
1. Call ui.inspect to locate the UICollectionView (capture accessibilityIdentifier or path)
2. Call ui_scroll with accessibilityIdentifier='<scrollview-id>', direction='up', amount optional (defaults to half visible region)
3. Call ui_wait mode='idle' stableMs=300 to let scroll layout settle
4. Call ui.inspect again to confirm new items are now visible in targets
5. Verify item set differs from pre-scroll inspect (new items appeared at bottom)

evals.prompt: Trigger a swipe-to-delete action on a UITableViewCell whose accessibility identifier is 'contact.cell.42'. The delete action title is '删除'.
evals.expected_output:
Should successfully:
1. Call ui_swipe with cellAccessibilityIdentifier='contact.cell.42', direction='left', actionTitle='删除'
2. direction='left' triggers trailing swipe actions (delete/archive is typical)
3. Response should indicate the swipe action was performed (not a container scroll)
4. Do NOT pass accessibilityIdentifier/path of the scroll view here — cellAccessibilityIdentifier switches ui_swipe to cell-action mode
5. Verify the row was deleted or action sheet / confirmation appeared

evals.prompt: Check the static structure of the ios-ui-list SKILL.md (offline, no app run): frontmatter must have name / description / allowed-tools fields present; every tool listed in allowed-tools must be a real iOSDriver MCP tool; the body must not hardcode SPMExample-specific values (com.coo.SPMExample bundle id, or specific UDIDs like 065CC8DB / 00008030).
evals.expected_output:
Should verify offline (no app run required):
1. Frontmatter has all three fields present — grep -E '^(name|description|allowed-tools):' returns 3
2. allowed-tools lists exactly these 7 real iOSDriver tools: mcp__iOSDriver__ui_inspect, mcp__iOSDriver__ui_scroll, mcp__iOSDriver__ui_scrollToElement, mcp__iOSDriver__ui_swipe, mcp__iOSDriver__ui_tap, mcp__iOSDriver__ui_tap_and_inspect, mcp__iOSDriver__ui_wait
3. No SPMExample hardcoding in body — grep -nE 'com\.coo\.SPMExample|065CC8DB|00008030' returns empty (OK)
4. name field value matches directory name (ios-ui-list)
5. description contains transition keyword '原 ios-list-interaction' for old-prompt routing
6. Body covers the four preserved capabilities: scrollToElement, ui_scroll (direction+amount), cell selection, cell swipe action
